# Predictive History — Volume II: Civilization

**Corpus:** `lectures/civilization-*.md`, `civ-*` in `metadata/sources.yaml`  
**Status:** Part I in progress (curated lectures); Part II procedure defined.

## Part I — Lecture-faithful chapters

One chapter per **Civilization** session, in classroom order, transcript-backed (same discipline as Volume I Part I). Chapter titles track lecture titles; canonical URLs live in `sources.yaml` and each lecture file.

**End-of-chapter box:** **Divergence (this lecture)** — not predictions. See [`CHAPTER-DIVERGENCE-BOX.md`](../CHAPTER-DIVERGENCE-BOX.md).

## Part II — Divergence

**Title:** [Part II — Divergence (Civilization)](PART-II-CIVILIZATION-DIVERGENCE.md)

Second pass: **historiography and divergence** — how Jiang’s claims compare to **named** mainstream or contested positions, using `divergence-tracking/registry/divergences.jsonl` and the method in [`divergence-tracking/README.md`](../divergence-tracking/README.md).
**Not** a forecast scorecard (contrast Volume I **Part II — Predictions** in [`PART-II-GEO-STRATEGY.md`](PART-II-GEO-STRATEGY.md)).

## Relation to Volume I

| Volume | Lecture series | Part I closes with | Part II |
|--------|----------------|--------------------|---------|
| I | Geo-Strategy | Prediction IDs (`predictions.jsonl`) | Prediction adjudication (news, triangulation) |
| II | Civilization | Divergence IDs (`divergences.jsonl`) | Historiographic divergence (sources, schools) |
